overview.description: Visual no-code/low-code platform for automating workflows and connecting apps
overview.description_long: Make (formerly Integromat) is a leading visual automation platform that enables individuals, teams, and enterprises to design, build, and automate workflows across 1,000+ apps and services without coding. Originally founded in Prague as Integromat in 2012, the company was acquired by Celonis in 2020 and rebranded to Make in 2022. With 500K+ users across 198 countries and 308 employees, Make serves as an enterprise-grade alternative to Zapier with more powerful visual workflow logic.

history.origin_story: Founded in Prague in 2012 as Integromat, a visual workflow automation platform described as an enterprise version of Zapier. Acquired by German process mining company Celonis in October 2020 and rebranded to Make in February 2022.

key_milestones.event: Acquired by Celonis (undisclosed amount); ~60 employees at time of acquisition
AWS partnership announced; listed on AWS Marketplace
Won Golden Kitty Award for Best No-Code Product

awards: Golden Kitty Award 2022 for Best No-Code Product

risk_assessment.agi_displacement_rationale: Make's visual workflow automation faces medium risk as LLMs increasingly enable natural language workflow creation that could bypass visual builders. However, Make's deep integration library (1,000+ apps), complex conditional logic capabilities, and visual debuggability provide a moat that is hard to replicate through pure language interfaces.
risk_assessment.market_risk_rationale: Make operates in an established and growing no-code automation market with 500K+ users and major enterprise clients like Spotify and Roche. Despite competition from Zapier, n8n, and Workato, Make has demonstrated strong product-market fit and differentiation through its visual interface and pricing.
risk_assessment.funding_risk_rationale: Make is a wholly-owned subsidiary of Celonis, Inc., which has raised over $2.4 billion. As an acquired company, Make benefits from Celonis' financial stability and is not subject to typical startup funding risk.
risk_assessment.overall_risk_rationale: Despite medium AGI displacement risk from natural language workflow alternatives, Make's low funding risk (backed by Celonis) and established user base of 500K+ across 198 countries with major enterprise clients provide strong overall resilience.

metadata.notes: Make.com website is Cloudflare-protected; data sourced from Wayback Machine archives. Employee count (308) from September 2024 Make About page. Founders identified from 2020 Integromat About page archive. Rebranded from Integromat to Make in February 2022 after Celonis acquisition in October 2020.
